topic: A person discovers their child has the same teacher they had
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp autumn air did little to settle the knot tightening in Sarah’s stomach. She clutched the enrollment packet, the glossy paper damp against her palm. It was the final confirmation for Leo’s first-grade placement, and the name printed boldly at the top made her breath hitch: Ms. Eleanor Vance. Ms. Vance. The same teacher who had nurtured Leo’s budding love for dinosaurs, the one whose gentle voice had soothed his early fears. A cold dread began to seep into Sarah’s bones. She reread the registration form, her eyes darting back and forth between the signature line and the teacher’s name. Every familiar memory of that classroom—the smell of old crayons and floor polish, the way Ms. Vance would laugh—suddenly felt tainted, overlaid with a heavy, unwelcome suspicion. She stood up abruptly, pacing the small living room, her knuckles white as she gripped the edge of the bookshelf. What if it was a coincidence? What if the universe was playing some cruel, elaborate joke?

[story 2]

Mark kept catching himself staring at the classroom photos on his phone. Leo, beaming in front of a colorful alphabet chart, looked so happy, so utterly himself. But then the caption appeared: "Class of 2023 – Ms. Vance." Mark’s jaw tightened. He felt a sudden, sharp clenching in his chest, a pressure building behind his ribs that made it hard to draw a full breath. He walked over to the window, pulling the curtain aside just a crack to look out at the street. He felt unsteady, as if the floor beneath him might shift. He remembered the day he first met Ms. Vance, the warmth of her greeting, and now, this connection felt dangerously intimate, almost predetermined. He turned back to the packet, his hands trembling slightly as he tried to focus on the required paperwork, but his mind kept looping back to that single, unsettling parallel.

[story 3]

“Are you sure about this, honey?” Sarah asked, her voice sounding strangely high-pitched even to her own ears. Leo just shrugged, a small, unconcerned gesture that only amplified the churning in Sarah’s gut. She watched him for a long moment, noting the casual way he tucked a stray piece of hair behind his ear. Then, the realization hit her with the force of a physical blow. Ms. Vance. The teacher who had been such a pillar of support for Leo. A wave of disorientation washed over her, leaving her feeling strangely hollowed out. She began to shuffle her feet on the rug, a restless energy vibrating through her limbs. She needed to call the school, needed some reassurance, but the words caught in her throat, thick and useless. The silence in the room seemed to amplify the frantic drumming of her own pulse.
